title: pedicularis sudetica subsp. scopulorum
intro: Pedicularis sudetica subsp. scopulorum, also known as Western lousewort, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the pedicularis genus and is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to provide various health benefits. The medicinal properties of Pedicularis sudetica subsp. scopulorum have been used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections, among others. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are typically used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
